title: Manually Jog The Tipper End Effector From The Operator Station HMI
summary: Use the operator station HMI jogging controls to manually move the left or right tipper end effector into position for maintenance procedures by stopping the automatic tipping cycle, opening the jogging screen, selecting the required axis, and using the plus or minus controls to move it in the documented direction.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the tipper end effector must be manually moved into position from the operator station HMI during maintenance procedures.

responsibilities: Navigate to the operator station HMI jogging interface.
Stop the automatic tipping cycle before opening the jogging screen.
Select the correct tipper axis and jog it using the documented direction controls.
Avoid driving an axis into a hard stop because doing so registers a fault.

safety_notes: This procedure is used for maintenance procedures and involves manual axis movement from the HMI.
Press CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle before opening the jogging screen.
If you push an axis until it hits a hard stop, the axis will register a fault.
access_or_tools_needed: HMI controls for CYCLE STOP and JOG

system_preconditions: Automatic tipping cycle can be stopped from the operator station HMI before jogging.
Manual Mode jogging controls are available on the jogging screen.

steps.title: Open the Visu_Dual_MCP screen
steps.purpose: Access the operator station HMI screen used to begin manual tipper jogging.
steps.instruction: On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen using F3.
steps.expected_result: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen is displayed on the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station HMI shows the Visu_Dual_MCP screen and the operator can access the controls needed for the next step.
steps.failure_condition: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen cannot be reached or the expected operator station controls are not available.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen cannot be opened from the operator station HMI.
The expected operator station HMI controls are not visible after navigation.

steps.title: Stop the automatic tipping cycle
steps.purpose: Place the station in the documented state before opening the jogging screen.
steps.instruction: Press CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle.
steps.expected_result: The automatic tipping cycle is stopped.
steps.healthy_condition: The tipping cycle is stopped and the station is ready for entry into the jogging screen.
steps.failure_condition: The tipping cycle does not stop or the CYCLE STOP control is not available.

actions.label: Press CYCLE STOP
actions.exact_action: Press CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle.

Press CYCLE STOP to stop the tipping cycle.

steps.instruction: Press JOG to open the jogging screen.
steps.expected_result: The jogging screen opens and Manual Mode jogging controls are available.
steps.healthy_condition: The jogging screen is displayed and the operator can see the axis selection and movement controls.
steps.failure_condition: The jogging screen does not open or the expected controls are not present.

actions.label: Press JOG
actions.exact_action: Press JOG to open the jogging screen.

steps.title: Identify Manual Mode axis and jog controls
steps.purpose: Confirm the operator is using the correct controls before moving any axis.
steps.instruction: On the jogging screen, identify the Manual Mode jogging controls for Z1 AXIS, Z2 AXIS, A1 AXIS, A2 AXIS, and the + and – buttons.
steps.expected_result: The operator can locate the axis selection buttons and the plus/minus movement controls.
steps.healthy_condition: Manual Mode is visible and the Z1, Z2, A1, A2, +, and – controls can be identified on the jogging screen.
steps.failure_condition: The required Manual Mode controls cannot be identified on the jogging screen.

actions.label: Identify Manual Mode controls
actions.exact_action: Identify the Manual Mode jogging controls for Z1 AXIS, Z2 AXIS, A1 AXIS, A2 AXIS, and the + and – buttons.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Manual Mode jogging screen showing Z1 AXIS, Z2 AXIS, A1 AXIS, A2 AXIS, and plus/minus controls.

Related operator station HMI snippet showing axis controls and Manual Mode elements.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Manual Mode controls are missing or unclear.
The operator cannot positively identify the intended axis selection and movement controls.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Z1 AXIS (vertical movement) and A1 AXIS (rotate the gripper) buttons move the left tipper; the Z2 AXIS and A2 AXIS buttons move the right tipper.
The + button moves the Z-axis up and rotates the A-axis towards the operator. The – button moves the Z-axis down and rotates the A-axis away from the operator.

steps.title: Select the axis for the required tipper movement
steps.purpose: Choose the correct axis based on whether the left or right tipper and vertical or rotational movement is needed.
steps.instruction: Select the axis to move: use Z1 AXIS for left tipper vertical movement, A1 AXIS for left tipper gripper rotation, Z2 AXIS for right tipper vertical movement, or A2 AXIS for right tipper movement.
steps.expected_result: The correct axis is selected for the intended manual movement.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected axis matches the intended side and movement type.
steps.failure_condition: The wrong axis is selected or the intended axis cannot be selected.

actions.label: Select tipper axis
actions.exact_action: Use Z1 AXIS for left tipper vertical movement, A1 AXIS for left tipper gripper rotation, Z2 AXIS for right tipper vertical movement, or A2 AXIS for right tipper movement.

steps.title: Jog the selected axis with the plus or minus control
steps.purpose: Manually move the selected tipper axis into the required position.
steps.instruction: Use the + or – button to move the selected axis.
steps.expected_result: The selected axis moves from the HMI jogging control input.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected axis responds to the chosen jog input and moves in the expected direction for positioning.
steps.failure_condition: The selected axis does not move as commanded, moves unexpectedly, or is driven into a hard stop and faults.

actions.label: Jog selected axis
actions.exact_action: Use the + or – button to move the selected axis.

stop_or_escalate_if: The selected axis does not move when jog input is applied.
The axis is pushed until it hits a hard stop.

steps.title: Verify Z-axis jog direction
steps.purpose: Confirm the vertical movement direction matches the documented jog behavior.
steps.instruction: For Z-axis movement, verify that + moves the axis up and – moves the axis down.
steps.expected_result: Z-axis movement direction matches the documented behavior.
steps.healthy_condition: Pressing + moves the Z-axis up and pressing – moves the Z-axis down.
steps.failure_condition: Z-axis movement does not match the documented direction.

actions.exact_action: Verify that + moves the Z-axis up and – moves the Z-axis down.

steps.instruction: For A-axis movement, verify that + rotates the axis towards the operator and – rotates the axis away from the operator.

steps.healthy_condition: Pressing + rotates the A-axis towards the operator and pressing – rotates the A-axis away from the operator.

success_criteria: The operator station HMI jogging screen is opened from Visu_Dual_MCP.
The automatic tipping cycle is stopped before jogging.
The correct left or right tipper axis is selected.
The selected axis moves using the plus or minus controls.
Z-axis movement follows the documented direction: + up, – down.
A-axis movement follows the documented direction: + towards the operator, – away from the operator.
The end effector is manually positioned as intended.
healthy_conditions: Visu_Dual_MCP is accessible from the operator station HMI using F3.
CYCLE STOP stops the automatic tipping cycle.
Manual Mode controls for Z1, Z2, A1, A2, +, and – are visible.
The selected axis responds to jog input.
Motion direction matches the documented behavior for Z and A axes.
No hard stop fault is registered during jogging.
failure_conditions: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen cannot be accessed.
CYCLE STOP does not stop the automatic tipping cycle.
JOG does not open the jogging screen.
Manual Mode axis controls cannot be identified.
The wrong axis is selected for the intended movement.
The selected axis does not move or moves unexpectedly.
Z-axis or A-axis movement direction does not match the documented behavior.
An axis is pushed into a hard stop and registers a fault.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if the required HMI screen or controls are unavailable.
Stop and escalate if the automatic tipping cycle cannot be stopped with CYCLE STOP.
Stop and escalate if the selected axis does not respond correctly to jog input.
Stop and escalate if motion direction does not match the documented behavior.
Stop and escalate if an axis hits a hard stop or registers a fault.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The end effector can be manually moved into position for maintenance procedures using the HMI.
On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen (F3).
Press CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle. Press JOG to open the jogging screen.
The Z1 AXIS (vertical movement) and A1 AXIS (rotate the gripper) buttons move the left tipper; the Z2 AXIS and A2 AXIS buttons move the right tipper.
The + button moves the Z-axis up and rotates the A-axis towards the operator. The – button moves the Z-axis down and rotates the A-axis away from the operator.
If you push the axis until it hits a hard stop, the axis will register a fault.

missing_details: The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not state whether production stop is formally required beyond stopping the automatic tipping cycle.
The source does not state whether lockout/tagout is required.
The source does not provide explicit completion confirmation beyond manual positioning and documented movement direction.
The source does not provide explicit recovery steps if an axis fault occurs after hitting a hard stop within this section.
